title: Add scalable archive evidence index
outOfScope: Changing the archival evidence schema beyond adding an index entry
Deleting, compressing, or rewriting existing archived evidence

sources.reason: Existing archive flow and sequence implementation.
Distribution and release evidence documentation boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: The index is additive metadata; existing Contract, Summary, and archive files remain authoritative.

executionDecision.reason: The index is a backward-compatible additive archive optimization.

preReviewWarnings: Review index entries, sequence monotonicity, and rollback behavior when index writes fail.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Archiving appends a machine-readable index entry without changing existing evidence files.
Index entries include Work Item identity, archive sequence, relative evidence paths, and archive year.
Index updates are atomic and archive failures restore the pre-archive file set.
Unit tests cover new-index, existing-index, and index-write failure paths.
Documentation explains the index as an acceleration layer, not a source of truth.
guidelines: The archive index must not replace the original Contract or Summary as authoritative evidence.
Index writes must be atomic and must not leave partial generated state.

intent.problem: The archive now contains hundreds of evidence files, so consumers that need a list of historical Work Items must repeatedly scan and parse the full archive.
constraints: Preserve all existing archive files and their paths.
Keep Contract and Summary as the authoritative evidence sources.
Do not add machine-specific paths or secrets to generated index data.
intent.rationale: An append-oriented index provides fast discovery while retaining the existing immutable evidence model.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
